channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: ICE officers set to deploy to airports as delays mount, border czar Homan confirms
item.description: Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.
item.content:encoded:
Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: This lab that's determined to discover new drugs isn't where you might expect
item.description: A scientist from Zambia who loves — LOVES! — chemistry runs a lab in South Africa that is being hailed for "extraordinary" work.
item.content:encoded:
A scientist from Zambia who loves — LOVES! — chemistry runs a lab in South Africa that is being hailed for "extraordinary" work.

(Image credit: Tommy Trenchard for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Brainteaser
item.description: NPR'S Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KXJZ listener Jen Hart and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR'S Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KXJZ listener Jen Hart and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants as Iran strikes 2 Israeli cities
item.description: Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Cortina d'Ampezzo mixes Olympic legacy with Alpine glamour
item.description: Cortina d'Ampezzo, the "Pearl of the Dolomites," is a blend of Olympic heritage with celebrity chic, fine dining and Alpine tradition, even as climate change and new tourism reshape the area.
item.content:encoded:
Cortina d'Ampezzo, the "Pearl of the Dolomites," is a blend of Olympic heritage with celebrity chic, fine dining and Alpine tradition, even as climate change and new tourism reshape the area.

(Image credit: Valerio Muscella for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: Cuba begins to restore power after third nationwide collapse in a month
item.description: Cuba's power grid collapsed Saturday leaving the country without electricity for a third time in March as the communist government battles with a decaying infrastructure and a U.S.-imposed oil blockade.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba's power grid collapsed Saturday leaving the country without electricity for a third time in March as the communist government battles with a decaying infrastructure and a U.S.-imposed oil blockade.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A strike on a hospital in Sudan killed at least 64 people, WHO says
item.description: At least 64 people were killed, including at least 13 children, in a strike on a hospital in Sudan's western Darfur region last week, the World Health Organization said Saturday.
item.content:encoded: At least 64 people were killed, including at least 13 children, in a strike on a hospital in Sudan's western Darfur region last week, the World Health Organization said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How one Minnesota school is bouncing back after the ICE surge
item.description: NPR spent time inside a Minnesota school talking with educators, parents, and children as it tries to help kids feel safe again after the ICE surge.
item.content:encoded:
NPR spent time inside a Minnesota school talking with educators, parents, and children as it tries to help kids feel safe again after the ICE surge.

(Image credit: Tim Evans for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Airport security lines are long. Here's what to know if you're flying
item.description: Travel experts say passengers need to be prepared, and patient, amid the government shutdown. Until a deal is reached, officials say airport disruptions and delays could get even worse.
item.content:encoded:
Travel experts say passengers need to be prepared, and patient, amid the government shutdown. Until a deal is reached, officials say airport disruptions and delays could get even worse.

(Image credit: Ronaldo Schemidt)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Robert Mueller, ex-FBI director who led 2016 Russia inquiry, dies at 81
item.description: Mueller's family told The New York Times in August that he had been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease.
item.content:encoded:
Mueller's family told The New York Times in August that he had been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster
